mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 3.64% provides room for easing
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during easing periods not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a 2-month window, signaling labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors front-run recession concerns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.3% is near trigger threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized rate while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously expand, indicating robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 757085M suggests sustained consumer demand
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during joint expansion periods not significantly higher than single-series baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 3.5%, creating stagflationary conditions that compress profit margins and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 3.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 102.50 and CPIAUCSL at 332.41
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) when tested against single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1.4 million units annualized rate, signaling weakening residential investment demand that portends broader economic slowdown and negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1.4 million units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1502.0 thousand units is above trigger
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns following HOUST declines not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100,000 jobs per month over a 3-month rolling average, indicating labor market slack that reduces consumer spending power and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month rolling average growth < 100,000 jobs per month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 62% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 4.5% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth remains above 3.0%, indicating restrictive monetary policy in a high-inflation regime that suppresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 3.64% below trigger threshold
regime_hint: restrictive monetary tightening with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) relative to additive single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 1.0% month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable below 3.0%, indicating healthy supply-side growth without cost pressures that supports profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 1.0% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth < 3.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 102.50 and CPIAUCSL at 332.41
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during joint expansion periods not significantly higher than single-series baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
